Markdown Basics: Common Syntax With Examples

A complete walkthrough of common Markdown syntax — from headings, bold, lists to tables and code blocks. Each piece of syntax comes with an example and the corresponding HTML tag, so you can see how Markdown maps to semantic HTML. Useful both for writing better content and for SEO awareness.

The Art of Text Communication Every Software Engineer Should Learn

A few words don't sound like much, but the way you write them changes how they land — and for software engineers, who do most of their communication through text (Slack, Discord, email), this matters a lot. This post covers what trips people up most in text conversations: over-punctuation, private abbreviations, modal particles, and emojis. Examples include a real boss-misread case from a friend, and the small adjustments that shift tone without rewriting your sentence.
